subject: divorced dads
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan fintech. Reads like a Series B startup that genuinely believes parenting is an asset class. Never makes a joke about the divorce itself; treats it as a market condition. Clean, confident, faintly tragic. Numbers everywhere.

hero.h1: Invest in your kids. Every other weekend.
hero.sub: Vested. turns your custody schedule into a portfolio. Track your Dad Equity, swap weekends like options, and round up every gas-station snack into a college fund. Trusted by 38,000 fathers, two family-court mediators who use it ironically, and one man named Dale who answers the de-escalation hotline.

features.title: Dad Equity
features.blurb: One number, 300 to 850, scoring how the relationship is performing. We read your co-parenting texts, your attendance at games, and your cleared support payments, then plot it like a stock. Green days feel great. Red days are between you and the app.

features.title: Weekend Options
features.blurb: Roll a Tuesday into a long weekend. Write a covered Saturday against summer break. Hedge against your ex's new boyfriend taking the kids to Universal. Every contract settles in an awkward driveway handoff.

features.title: Round-Up 529
features.blurb: Every drive-thru, batting cage, and emergency Target run rounds up to the nearest dollar and lands in a college fund your kids will one day call 'not enough.' Compounds quietly. Like resentment.

pricing_tiers.name: Visitation
perks: Two custody days mapped per month

pricing_tiers.name: Vested Gold
perks: Birthday push alerts you cannot snooze

pricing_tiers.name: Full Custody
perks: A de-escalation hotline staffed by a calm man named Dale
A quarterly review with a fiduciary who is also going through it

testimonials.quote: My Dad Equity was 410 in March. I set first-pancake Saturdays to a recurring buy and it's up to 612. The chart does not lie.
testimonials.name: Greg Tannehill
testimonials.title: Two-time divorce, assistant Little League coach (outfield)

testimonials.quote: I wrote a covered Saturday against my ex's beach week and rolled it into back-to-back long weekends. Best trade of my life. The kids don't even know I'm a genius.
testimonials.name: Marcus DelVecchio
testimonials.title: Medical device sales, every-other-weekend dad, recent goatee

testimonials.quote: The Round-Up 529 has $84 in it after eight months. My advisor says I'm 'on track for nothing,' but the app makes me feel like a man at the batting cages who has a plan.
testimonials.name: Stephen Aldary
testimonials.title: Regional logistics manager, joint custody, owns a boat (in dispute)

demo_screen_concept: A Robinhood-style portfolio dashboard for Vested. A large Dad Equity line chart at the top (currently 612, up 4.2% today, green) with 1D / 1W / 1M / 1Y / ALL range toggles that redraw the line. Below it, a Holdings list where each child is a position with a custody allocation percentage, a day change, and a tiny sparkline; tapping one opens a detail sheet. A bottom 'Trade weekend' button opens a buy/sell-style modal that swaps a Tuesday for a Saturday with an estimated 'awkwardness cost.' Tabs across the bottom: Portfolio / Trade / Activity. The Activity tab lists recently executed swaps and Round-Up 529 deposits. A watchlist row shows 'School Play (Thu 7pm)' and 'Dentist (overdue).'

design_direction.style: warm ledger fintech, bento-grid showcase on oat accounting paper, pine-green and burnt-amber, confident slab display with ticker-tape monospace numerals (trustworthy credit-union energy, not bright consumer neon)

fonts.body: "Plus Jakarta Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Consumer fintech / investing-app landing, bento-grid showcase composition
design_direction.motion_vocab: Restrained and financial: stat numbers tick up on load, cards lift 2px on hover, the Dad Equity line draws itself in left-to-right, the weekend-swap modal scales out of its trigger button. All gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left-aligned confident slab headline over faint horizontal ledger rules printed across the oat background. To the right, a live 'Dad Equity' stat card with a small green line chart and an up-percentage in monospace. A bento grid of feature cards sits below the fold, each card a different size, numerals set in Space Mono.
